-- stg_customers.sql
-- Cleans DimCustomer records

with source as (
    select * from "postgres"."dmql_base"."DimCustomer"
),

cleaned as (
    select
        "CustomerID"                        as customer_id,
        trim("FullName")                    as full_name,
        "DOB"                               as date_of_birth,
        trim("Gender")                      as gender,
        trim("Region")                      as region,
        lower(trim("Email"))                as email,
        upper(trim("Status"))               as status,
        "JoinDate"                          as join_date,
        ingested_at
    from source
    where "CustomerID" is not null
)

select * from cleaned

-- stg_accounts.sql
-- Cleans DimAccount records

with source as (
    select * from "postgres"."dmql_base"."DimAccount"
),

cleaned as (
    select
        "AccountID"                         as account_id,
        "CustomerID"                        as customer_id,
        trim("AccountType")                 as account_type,
        "OpenDate"                          as open_date,
        "ClosedDate"                        as closed_date,
        upper(trim("Status"))               as status,
        "RegistrationID"                    as registration_id,
        "Balance"::decimal(15, 2)           as balance,
        ingested_at
    from source
    where "AccountID" is not null
)

select * from cleaned

-- stg_products.sql
-- Joins DimProduct → DimProductSubCategory → DimProductCategory into one flat staging model

with products as (
    select * from "postgres"."dmql_base"."DimProduct"
),

subcategories as (
    select * from "postgres"."dmql_base"."DimProductSubCategory"
),

categories as (
    select * from "postgres"."dmql_base"."DimProductCategory"
),

joined as (
    select
        p."ProductID"                           as product_id,
        trim(p."ProductName")                   as product_name,
        s."ProductSubCategoryID"                as sub_category_id,
        trim(s."ProductSubCategoryName")        as sub_category_name,
        c."ProductCategoryID"                   as category_id,
        trim(c."ProductCategoryName")           as category_name
    from products p
    left join subcategories s on p."ProductSubcategoryID" = s."ProductSubCategoryID"
    left join categories c on s."ProductCategoryID" = c."ProductCategoryID"
    where p."ProductID" is not null
)

select * from joined

-- stg_transactions.sql
-- Cleans and standardizes raw FactTransaction records

with source as (
    select * from "postgres"."dmql_base"."FactTransaction"
),

cleaned as (
    select
        "TransactionID"                             as transaction_id,
        "AccountID"                                 as account_id,
        "ProductID"                                 as product_id,
        cast("TransactionDate" as date)             as transaction_date,
        "TransactionAmount"::decimal(15, 2)         as transaction_amount,
        upper(trim("TransactionType"))              as transaction_type,
        upper(trim("TransactionChannel"))           as transaction_channel,
        upper(trim("Status"))                       as status,
        ingested_at
    from source
    where "TransactionID" is not null
      and "AccountID" is not null
      and "TransactionAmount" is not null
)

select * from cleaned

-- dim_customer.sql
-- Final customer dimension for the Star Schema

with customers as (
    select * from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_customers"
)

select
    customer_id,
    full_name,
    date_of_birth,
    gender,
    region,
    email,
    status,
    join_date,
    -- Derived: customer age bucket
    case
        when date_of_birth is null then 'Unknown'
        when date_part('year', age(to_date(date_of_birth, 'DD/MM/YYYY'))) < 25 then '18-24'
        when date_part('year', age(to_date(date_of_birth, 'DD/MM/YYYY'))) < 35 then '25-34'
        when date_part('year', age(to_date(date_of_birth, 'DD/MM/YYYY'))) < 50 then '35-49'
        when date_part('year', age(to_date(date_of_birth, 'DD/MM/YYYY'))) < 65 then '50-64'
        else '65+'
    end as age_bucket
from customers

select
    customer_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_customers"
where customer_id is not null
group by customer_id
having count(*) > 1

select full_name
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_customers"
where full_name is null

select customer_id
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_customers"
where customer_id is null

select customer_id
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_accounts"
where customer_id is null

select account_id
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_accounts"
where account_id is null

select
    account_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_accounts"
where account_id is not null
group by account_id
having count(*) > 1

-- dim_product.sql
-- Flattened product dimension with category hierarchy

with products as (
    select * from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_products"
)

select
    product_id,
    product_name,
    sub_category_id,
    sub_category_name,
    category_id,
    category_name,
    -- Derived: full hierarchy label
    category_name || ' > ' || sub_category_name || ' > ' || product_name as product_hierarchy
from products

select
    product_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_products"
where product_id is not null
group by product_id
having count(*) > 1

select product_name
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_products"
where product_name is null

select product_id
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_products"
where product_id is null

-- dim_date.sql
-- Date dimension generated from the range of transaction dates

with date_spine as (
    select generate_series(
        (select min(transaction_date) from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"),
        (select max(transaction_date) from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"),
        interval '1 day'
    )::date as full_date
)

select
    full_date                                           as date_key,
    full_date,
    date_part('year',  full_date)::int                  as year,
    date_part('month', full_date)::int                  as month,
    to_char(full_date, 'Month')                         as month_name,
    date_part('day',   full_date)::int                  as day,
    date_part('dow',   full_date)::int                  as day_of_week,   -- 0=Sun
    to_char(full_date, 'Day')                           as day_name,
    date_part('quarter', full_date)::int                as quarter,
    'Q' || date_part('quarter', full_date)::int         as quarter_label,
    date_part('week', full_date)::int                   as week_of_year,
    case
        when date_part('dow', full_date) in (0, 6) then true
        else false
    end                                                 as is_weekend
from date_spine

with all_values as (

    select
        status as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
    group by status

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'SUCCESS','FAILED'
)

select account_id
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
where account_id is null

select transaction_amount
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
where transaction_amount is null

select transaction_date
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
where transaction_date is null

select transaction_id
from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
where transaction_id is null

select
    transaction_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
where transaction_id is not null
group by transaction_id
having count(*) > 1

-- dim_account.sql
-- Account dimension enriched with customer info

with accounts as (
    select * from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_accounts"
),

customers as (
    select customer_id, full_name, region, status as customer_status
    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_customer"
)

select
    a.account_id,
    a.customer_id,
    c.full_name          as customer_name,
    c.region             as customer_region,
    a.account_type,
    a.open_date,
    a.closed_date,
    a.status             as account_status,
    c.customer_status,
    a.registration_id,
    a.balance,
    -- Derived: account age in days
    current_date - a.open_date as account_age_days
from accounts a
left join customers c using (customer_id)

with all_values as (

    select
        status as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_customer"
    group by status

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'ACTIVE','INACTIVE','SUSPENDED'
)

select customer_id
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_customer"
where customer_id is null

select full_name
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_customer"
where full_name is null

select
    customer_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_customer"
where customer_id is not null
group by customer_id
having count(*) > 1

select category_name
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_product"
where category_name is null

select product_id
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_product"
where product_id is null

where product_name is null

select
    product_id as unique_field,

from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_product"
where product_id is not null
group by product_id
having count(*) > 1

select date_key

-- fact_transactions.sql
-- Core fact table joining all dimensions into the Star Schema

with transactions as (
    select * from "postgres"."analytics_staging"."stg_transactions"
),

accounts as (
    select account_id, customer_id, account_type, account_status, customer_region
    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
),

products as (
    select product_id, product_name, sub_category_name, category_name
    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_product"
),

dates as (
    select date_key, year, month, quarter, quarter_label, is_weekend
    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_date"
)

select
    -- Keys
    t.transaction_id,
    t.account_id,
    a.customer_id,
    t.product_id,
    t.transaction_date                  as date_key,

    -- Descriptors (denormalized for BI performance)
    t.transaction_type,
    t.transaction_channel,
    t.status                            as transaction_status,
    a.account_type,
    a.account_status,
    a.customer_region,
    p.product_name,
    p.sub_category_name,
    p.category_name,
    d.year,
    d.month,
    d.quarter,
    d.quarter_label,
    d.is_weekend,

    -- Measures
    t.transaction_amount,

    -- Derived flags
    case when t.status = 'FAILED'    then 1 else 0 end as is_failed,
    case when t.status = 'COMPLETED' then 1 else 0 end as is_completed,
    case when d.is_weekend = true    then 1 else 0 end as is_weekend_txn

from transactions t
left join accounts a using (account_id)
left join products p using (product_id)
left join dates d on t.transaction_date = d.date_key

with all_values as (

    select
        account_status as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
    group by account_status

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'OPEN','CLOSED'
)

select account_id
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
where account_id is null

select balance
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
where balance is null

select customer_id
from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
where customer_id is null

with child as (
    select customer_id as from_field
    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
    where customer_id is not null
),

parent as (
    select customer_id as to_field
    from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_customer"
)

select
    from_field

from child
left join parent
    on child.from_field = parent.to_field

where parent.to_field is null

select
    account_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "postgres"."analytics_analytics"."dim_account"
where account_id is not null
group by account_id
having count(*) > 1
